summary: Decompiled additional Castle stage (`grcastle`) pieces: two callback tables, several small wrapper/stub-like functions, a partially understood `unkCastle` struct, and typed prototypes for castle callbacks and helpers. The PR also moved castle-related forward declarations into `gr/forward.h` after review clarified where the incomplete type and callback typedefs should live.
what_changed: Added `unkCastleCallback grCs_803B7F28[]` and `unkCastleCallback2 grCs_803B7F3C[]` tables in `grcastle.c`, each containing five castle callback functions.

Implemented `grCastle_801CD338`, which gets a ground gobj with `Ground_801C2BA4(6)`, fetches a JObj with `Ground_801C3FA4(gobj, 4)`, and hides it with `HSD_JObjSetFlagsAll(..., JOBJ_HIDDEN)` when present.

Implemented small castle functions including `grCastle_801CD4A4` calling `grZakoGenerator_801CAE04(0)`, `grCastle_801D0298` calling `grCastle_801CFBD4()`, `grCastle_801D0A9C` adjusting an `HSD_Generator` app-srt position by a scale factor, and `grCastle_801D0FF0` returning `50.0f * Ground_801C0498()`.
Replaced several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes in `grcastle.h` with typed signatures for `grCastle_801D0550` through `grCastle_801D08AC`, `grCastle_801D0A9C`, and `grCastle_801D0FF0`.
Introduced `struct unkCastle` in `grcastle.h` with known fields at offsets `0x10C` (`HSD_GObj* x10C[5]`) and `0x134` (`u8 x134[5]`), preserving unknown padding elsewhere.
Added forward declaration `typedef struct unkCastle unkCastle;` and callback typedefs `unkCastleCallback` and `unkCastleCallback2` to `gr/forward.h`.
Renamed many `Ground_GObj* arg` parameters in already-present trivial functions to `gobj`, improving local clarity without changing behavior.
Used shared helper functions `grCastle_801D0550_sub` and `grCastle_801D06CC_sub` to implement five nearly identical castle callbacks each, indexed from 0 to 4.
smart_moves: Factored repeated five-way callback behavior into indexed helper functions instead of duplicating the full body for each address-specific callback. This made the table-driven pattern explicit while retaining the individual function symbols required for matching.
Kept unknown structure regions as padding and only named the fields directly required by the matched code (`x10C` and `x134`), avoiding overclaiming the meaning of the castle data layout.
Typed callback arrays and function prototypes together, making the table entries and function definitions agree instead of leaving them as generic `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS`.
Used `PAD_STACK(16)` in the `grCastle_801D0550`-family wrappers, indicating attention to stack-shape matching for otherwise tiny wrapper functions.
Moved common incomplete-type and callback declarations to `gr/forward.h` so other ground/stage headers can reference `unkCastle` without needing the full struct definition.
decomp_lessons: For stage-specific structs, put the full struct definition in the owning stage header when its fields are needed there, but put the incomplete typedef in `gr/forward.h` when callback typedefs or other headers only need a pointer type.
When decompiling address-family callbacks that differ only by a small index, an inline/static helper plus thin wrappers can express the repeated behavior and preserve separate function symbols.
Do not fully reverse a struct just to make progress; padded unknown regions with verified offsets are acceptable when only a few fields are needed for matching.
Callback table decomp often forces header cleanup: table element typedefs, function prototypes, and forward declarations need to be made consistent together.
Small matching functions may need explicit stack padding even if their semantic body is just a call through a helper.
naming_conventions: Castle stage symbols retain the project’s address-based names, e.g. `grCastle_801D0550`, `grCastle_801D06CC`, and `grCastle_801D0FF0`.
Static/common helper names were formed by appending `_sub` to the first function in a repeated family, e.g. `grCastle_801D0550_sub` and `grCastle_801D06CC_sub`.
The newly introduced, not-yet-understood castle struct is named `unkCastle`, reflecting incomplete semantics rather than an invented gameplay name.
Callback typedefs follow the unknown-struct name: `unkCastleCallback` and `unkCastleCallback2`.
Generic `arg` parameters were renamed to `gobj` where the type is `Ground_GObj*`, improving readability while preserving unknown function names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK(16)` in five thin callbacks (`grCastle_801D0550`, `grCastle_801D059C`, `grCastle_801D05E8`, `grCastle_801D0634`, `grCastle_801D0680`) to satisfy stack layout/matching needs.
Represented two contiguous five-entry data tables as typed callback arrays (`grCs_803B7F28`, `grCs_803B7F3C`) instead of opaque data.
Matched five similar no-`gobj` callbacks through an inline helper that sets `x134[i]`, calls `grMaterial_801C8CDC(x10C[i])`, clears the pointer, and calls `Ground_801C53EC(0x53020U)`.
Matched five similar collision/object callbacks through a static helper gated by `(ftLib_80086960(gobj)) || (itGetKind(gobj) != 0xA0)`, then performing the same material/flag/pointer-clear update.
Preserved exact known struct offsets with explicit padding arrays (`_pad[0x10C]`, `_pad2[0x134-0x120]`) to make generated loads/stores line up.
review_feedback: Author questioned whether `unkCastle` structs/functions belonged in `grcastle.h`: "not sure if this is the right place to define these structs/functions...?"
Reviewer ribbanya initially advised using `gr/forward.h` and forward-declaring `unkCastle` there.

Reviewer then clarified the direction: the callback typedefs should go in `forward`, while the full struct should remain in `grcastle.h`: "Other way around; this goes in `forward` and the struct goes here."
Author acknowledged the correction: "lol yup, just realized".
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further define `unkCastle` fields `x10C` and `x134`.
Check whether `grCs_803B7F28` and `grCs_803B7F3C` table names were later given semantic names.
Search for other stage files using the same five-callback indexed helper pattern for breakable objects or material changes.
Look for later cleanup of `UNK_T` versus `void*` in `unkCastleCallback` prototypes.
Investigate the meaning of item kind `0xA0` in the `grCastle_801D06CC_sub` condition.
